input: Choose the correct option (A–D): Which statement best characterizes RPL22L1’s role and mechanism in ovarian cancer and the consequence of its suppression? [C] RPL22L1 promotes colorectal cancer cell migration, invasion, and in vivo tumor progression by inducing epithelial-to-mesenchymal transition, and its suppression has no effect. [D] RPL22L1 promotes only tumor cell proliferation independent of epithelial-to-mesenchymal transition, and its suppression does not alter metastatic phenotypes.

RPL22L1 promotes ovarian cancer cell migration, invasion, and in vivo tumor progression by inducing epithelial-to-mesenchymal transition, and suppressing RPL22L1 diminishes these metastatic phenotypes.

Reason (R): Induction of epithelial-to-mesenchymal transition increases motility and invasiveness that drive tumor progression, so reducing EMT through RPL22L1 suppression curtails these metastatic behaviors.

input: Choose the correct option (A–D): Which statement correctly describes FBP1's role in gastric cancer with respect to inhibiting epithelial-mesenchymal transition, the effects of reduced FBP1 expression on proliferation, migration, and invasion, its correlations with nodal and lymphovascular involvement and survival, and whether it serves as an independent prognostic biomarker? Assertion (A): In gastric cancer, FBP1 functions as a tumor suppressor by inhibiting epithelial-mesenchymal transition, and reduced FBP1 expression enhances proliferation, migration, and invasion and correlates with advanced nodal involvement, lymphovascular invasion, and poorer survival, thereby serving as an independent prognostic biomarker.
